technique: Credential stuffing / password spray -> breach pivot
tactic: TA0006 Credential Access -> TA0001 Initial Access

trigger.from_breadth: A UPN with >=10 auth FAILUREs in 24h, OR failures from >=5 distinct source countries (Surface-3 mandatory-basic lead)

telemetry_gap_behavior: If the stream has events but BOTH field conventions are null, sweep raw full_message/message for result codes 50126/50053/50074 + the UPN; NEVER emit clean off a null named field. If stream total=0/24h, emit REVIEW stream-empty (not clean). This null-field path is the exact gap that missed the DEV-GL stuffing 2026-06-15.

method.query: (azure_result_signature:FAILURE OR resultSignature:FAILURE) AND (azure_prop_user_principal_name:"<UPN>" OR properties_userPrincipalName:"<UPN>") AND NOT azure_result_type:(53003 70043 70044 50173 50140)
method.compute: total count; distinct source_ip_country_code; distinct callerIpAddress/source_ip; top azure_result_type (50126 wrong-pw / 50053 lockout / 50074 MFA-required); distinct properties_clientAppUsed
method.note: Legacy clientAppUsed (Authenticated SMTP, BAV2ROPC, IMAP4, POP3, Other clients) = MFA-bypass-capable -> raises ceiling

method.goal: THE PIVOT - any SUCCESS for this UPN in window (the catch breadth misses)
method.compute: success present? capture success source_ip, source_ip_country_code, appDisplayName, timestamp
method.decision_input: ANY success after a failure burst = breach-pivot candidate -> escalate

method.goal: Grade the success - MFA / token posture
method.fields: properties_authenticationRequirement (singleFactor vs multiFactor) ; properties_conditionalAccessStatus OR azure_prob_condi_access_status ; properties_incomingTokenType (primaryRefreshToken = PRT/replay) ; properties_tokenProtectionStatusDetails_signInSessionStatus (unbound = AiTM)
method.decision_input: success with singleFactor where MFA is required, OR unbound session token, OR PRT from a new IP = token replay / AiTM, not just a guessed password

method.check: success properties_clientAppUsed in {Authenticated SMTP, BAV2ROPC, IMAP4, POP3, Exchange ActiveSync, Other clients}
method.decision_input: legacy + success on an MFA-enforced user = confirmed MFA-bypass breach. NOTE per environment: this rule holds on casepoint.in (DEV-GL); on casepoint.com legacy SMTP is LEGIT for ~8 service accounts -> do not auto-fire there

method.compute: is the success IP/country inside the failure set, or brand-new?
method.decision_input: same-IP failure->success = classic stuffing success; new-country success = stronger compromise / impossible-travel -> chain to impossible-travel module

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: success after failure burst AND (legacy clientAppUsed on .in OR unbound token OR PRT-from-new-IP OR new-country)
decision.HIGH_conf4: success after failure burst from a known context but with NO MFA challenge where MFA is required
decision.MEDIUM_conf3to4: failures only (>=10 or >=5 countries), NO success = attempted spray/stuffing (still reported)
decision.FP_kill: success is MFA-satisfied from a known-good IP/device; failures explained by smart-lockout (50053) of a stale cred with no success

false_positive_checks: Cato egress 140.82.202.196 / 199.27.40.187 / 123.253.153.138 -> cap MEDIUM unless data export/admin action
smart-lockout-only (50053) with NO success = control working on stale cred -> MEDIUM attempted, not breach
casepoint.com service accounts with legitimate Authenticated SMTP (~8) -> legacy success EXPECTED there; SMTP-success=compromise applies to casepoint.in only
known historical good IP/device for the UPN (if baseline available)
